skill.name: meta-results-sensitivity-analysis
skill.description: Generates the Results section for meta-analysis sensitivity analysis based on statistical tables and titles. Use when the user wants to describe sensitivity analysis results or format sensitivity tables for a meta-analysis paper.
skill.summary: Generates formatted Results sections for meta-analysis sensitivity analysis from Leave-One-Out statistical tables.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 52 potential issues across 3 files (490 lines). After manual evaluation, all 52 findings are false positives. The 27 backtick execution flags are markdown code block formatting used for documentation examples. The 23 weak cryptographic algorithm flags are text pattern artifacts with no actual cryptographic operations in any file. The 2 hardcoded URL flags are legitimate source attribution links. The 3 system reconnaissance flags in the audit JSON are from structured audit data, not reconnaissance commands. The skill is a documentation-based academic writing aid with a trivial argparse-only Python script. Risk level set to low solely due to community source origin, not due to any confirmed security issues.

low_findings.title: Markdown code block formatting triggers backtick execution false positives
low_findings.description: All 27 backtick execution flags are markdown triple-backtick code blocks used for documenting example bash and python commands. These are instructional text, not executable code. No actual shell execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Text patterns trigger weak cryptographic algorithm false positives
low_findings.description: All 23 weak cryptographic algorithm flags are text pattern artifacts in markdown documentation, a trivial argparse script, and an audit JSON file. No file contains any cryptographic operations or hash functions.

content.user_title: Generate Meta-Analysis Sensitivity Analysis Results Sections
content.value_statement: Writing the Results section for sensitivity analysis in meta-analysis papers is time-consuming and requires precise statistical description. This skill automates the process by generating well-formatted textual descriptions from your Leave-One-Out sensitivity tables in either Chinese or English.
actual_capabilities: Generate textual descriptions of Leave-One-Out sensitivity analysis tables for meta-analysis papers
Format results sections with proper table citations and standard legends
Support output in both Chinese and English languages
Produce consistent and reproducible results section formatting across multiple outcomes
Integrate statistical table references with narrative academic text
Validate inputs before execution and surface missing parameters clearly
limitations: Requires user-provided statistical table data as input and cannot generate results without it
Cannot perform statistical calculations or verify the accuracy of provided data
Limited to Leave-One-Out sensitivity analysis method and does not support other sensitivity approaches
Output quality depends on completeness of input parameters including title and outcome name

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Writing Meta-Analysis Manuscript
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher preparing a meta-analysis paper needs to describe sensitivity analysis results in proper academic format with correct table citations.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Preparing Thesis Chapter
use_cases.description: A graduate student writing their thesis on meta-analysis needs formatted results sections for sensitivity checks with consistent formatting.

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Author Needing Consistent Reporting
use_cases.description: An author of a systematic review with meta-analysis needs uniform sensitivity analysis reporting across multiple outcome measures.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Sensitivity Results Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Results section describing the sensitivity analysis for my meta-analysis. The outcome is [outcome name] and the title is [meta-analysis title]. Here is the Leave-One-Out sensitivity table: [paste table data].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a complete sensitivity table and wants a straightforward textual description for their manuscript.

prompt_templates.title: English Language Results with Full Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the Results section for the sensitivity analysis of my meta-analysis in English. Title: [meta-analysis title]. Outcome: [outcome name]. Sensitivity method: Leave-One-Out. Here is the table data: [paste table]. Include proper table citation formatting.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs English output with all parameters explicitly specified for precise control.

prompt_templates.title: Chinese Language Results for Bilingual Publication
prompt_templates.prompt: 请用中文撰写我荟萃分析中敏感性分析的结果部分。荟萃分析标题：[title]。结局指标：[outcome name]。敏感性分析方法：留一法。表格数据如下：[paste table]。请包含正确的表格引用格式。
prompt_templates.scenario: User is publishing in a Chinese-language journal and needs results in Chinese with proper formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Outcome Sensitivity Reporting
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate sensitivity analysis Results sections for multiple outcomes in my meta-analysis. Title: [title]. Language: [Chinese or English]. For each outcome below, generate a separate results paragraph with table citations. Outcome 1: [name], table: [data]. Outcome 2: [name], table: [data]. Outcome 3: [name], table: [data].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs consistent sensitivity reporting across several outcomes in a single meta-analysis paper.

output_examples.input: Generate sensitivity analysis results for meta-analysis titled 'Efficacy of Drug A vs Placebo' with outcome 'Mortality Rate' using provided Leave-One-Out table.
output_examples.output: Sensitivity analysis using the Leave-One-Out method demonstrated that the overall effect estimate for mortality rate remained stable. When each individual study was sequentially removed from the analysis, the pooled odds ratio ranged from 0.72 to 0.81, and all recalculated confidence intervals excluded the null value of 1.0 (Table 5). These findings indicate that no single study disproportionately influenced the overall result, confirming the robustness of the primary meta-analysis findings.

output_examples.input: 请用中文撰写敏感性分析结果部分，标题为'他汀类药物与糖尿病风险'，结局指标为'新发糖尿病'。
output_examples.output: 留一法敏感性分析结果显示，新发糖尿病的合并效应估计值保持稳定。逐一剔除各项研究后，合并风险比在1.08至1.15之间波动，且所有重新计算的置信区间均未跨越1.0（表5）。上述结果表明，没有单一研究对总体结果产生不成比例的影响，证实了主要荟萃分析结果的稳健性。

best_practices: Always provide complete statistical table data to ensure accurate and detailed textual descriptions
Specify the output language explicitly using either Chinese or English to ensure correct formatting conventions
Review generated output for alignment with your target journal requirements before submission
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill without providing the underlying statistical table data as it cannot fabricate results
Do not expect the skill to perform statistical calculations or verify the accuracy of your input data
Do not submit generated output verbatim without human review for academic accuracy and completeness

faq.question: What input data does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires a Leave-One-Out sensitivity analysis table, the meta-analysis title, the outcome name, and the target output language (Chinese or English).

faq.question: Does this skill perform statistical calculations?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates textual descriptions from statistical tables you provide. It does not calculate statistics or verify your data.

faq.question: What languages are supported for output?
faq.answer: The skill supports Chinese and English output. You must specify which language you need when providing your input.

faq.question: Can this skill handle sensitivity methods other than Leave-One-Out?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for Leave-One-Out sensitivity analysis. Other sensitivity methods are not supported.

faq.question: What format does the output follow?
faq.answer: The output includes a narrative Results section paragraph with proper table citations inserted before the final punctuation, plus a formatted table legend.

faq.question: Is the generated output ready for publication?
faq.answer: The output provides a well-structured draft, but you should always review it for accuracy, completeness, and alignment with your specific journal guidelines before submission.
